title: Recommendation: Syphilis Infection During Pregnancy: Screening | United States Preventive Services Taskforce
text:
ion. 10 In its deliberation of the evidence, the USPSTF considers whether the new evidence is of sufficient strength and quality to change its previous conclusions about the evidence.
Using a reaffirmation process, the USPSTF concludes with high certainty that screening for syphilis infection in pregnancy has a substantial net benefit .
See Table 1 for more information on the USPSTF recommendation rationale and assessment. For more details on the methods the USPSTF uses to determine the net benefit, see the USPSTF Procedure Manual. 10 
Return to Table of Contents 
Practice Considerations
Patient Population Under Consideration 
This recommendation applies to all adolescents and adults who are pregnant, whether or not risk factors for syphilis are present.
Screening Tests 
Screening for syphilis involves a blood test that detects antibodies that may reflect infection with Treponema pallidum, the organism that causes syphilis. Treponemal tests, such as the T pallidum particle agglutination (TP-PA) test, detect an antibody response to antigens specific to T pallidum . 11 Nontreponemal tests, such as the Venereal Disease Research Laboratory or rapid plasma reagin test, detect antibodies that may reflect tissue damage from T pallidum infection or tissue damage from other conditions that can cause the release of lipoidal antigens.
